Regulatory update tracker

Community banks & fintechs · last updated July 18, 2026 22:06 UTC

Press Ctrl + D to bookmark this page

Read this first. The summaries are based on agency listings. They are a starting point for triage, not legal or compliance advice, and they can be wrong or incomplete. Always open the source document before acting on anything here. Deadlines shown are structured fields taken from matched Federal Register records; items without a match show none, which does not mean none exists.
What this covers, and what it does not

Tracked: the US federal banking and financial-crime agencies listed below. History depth varies by source — some publish archives going back years, others only their most recent items.

CFPB — 19 items, 2025-02-03 to 2026-06-25
CFPB Rules — 25 items, 2024-10-22 to 2026-06-08
CFTC — 10 items, 2026-06-23 to 2026-07-17
CSBS — 8 items, 2026-06-17 to 2026-07-15
FDIC — 25 items, 2026-05-01 to 2026-07-17
FDIC FILs — 25 items, 2026-04-17 to 2026-07-17
FTC — 10 items, 2026-07-02 to 2026-07-15
Fed SR/CA Letters — 15 items, 2023-08-08 to 2026-07-09
Federal Reserve — 20 items, 2026-06-11 to 2026-07-16
FinCEN — 101 items, 2021-12-08 to 2026-06-25
FinCEN Advisories — 74 items, 2000-08 to 2026-06-30
NCUA — 100 items, 2024-11-19 to 2026-07-16
NCUA Press — 60 items, 2025-12-23 to 2026-07-13
OCC — 10 items, 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-16
OCC Bulletins — 11 items, 2026-06-11 to 2026-07-16
OFAC — 10 items, 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-17

Not tracked: state regulators (including NYDFS and California DFPI, which block automated access), FFIEC, SEC, FTC and CFTC. Congressional activity and court decisions are not covered. Anything an agency published but did not list on the pages above will be missing.

Relevance: items are screened against a profile of US community banks (under ~$10B assets) and fintechs — BaaS and sponsor-bank arrangements, prepaid and FBO accounts, consumer lending and credit risk, BSA/AML, and internal audit. Items outside that scope are collected but filtered out, so this is not a complete record of everything these agencies publish.

Federal Reserve regulation reference (A–YY)

What each regulation letter covers, and where it now lives in the CFR. This is a lookup aid, not a citation source — confirm anything you intend to cite against the Federal Reserve's own regulation listing. Reserved and never-finalised letters are omitted.

Consumer compliance

Fair lending, deposits and consumer credit. Many moved to the CFPB after Dodd-Frank — same letter, new CFR home.

RegSubjectCFR
BEqual Credit Opportunity Act (fair lending)CFPB 1002
CHome Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA)CFPB 1003
EElectronic Fund Transfer ActCFPB 1005
FFair Debt Collection Practices ActCFPB 1006
GSAFE Act — MLO registration for bank employeesCFPB 1007
MConsumer Leasing ActCFPB 1013
PGLBA privacyCFPB 1016
VFair Credit Reporting ActCFPB 1022
XRESPACFPB 1024
ZTruth in LendingCFPB 1026
BBCommunity Reinvestment ActFed 228
CCFunds availability / Check 21Fed 229
DDTruth in SavingsCFPB 1030
FFUse of medical information in creditFed 232

Safety, soundness and structural

Retained by the Federal Reserve.

RegSubjectCFR
ADiscount window lending12 CFR 201
DReserve requirements12 CFR 204
HState member bank membership12 CFR 208
IReserve Bank capital stock12 CFR 209
JCheck collection and Fedwire12 CFR 210
KInternational banking operations12 CFR 211
LManagement official interlocks12 CFR 212
NRelations with foreign banks12 CFR 214
OInsider lending — officers, directors, principal shareholders12 CFR 215
QRegulatory capital (Basel III minimums)12 CFR 217
RBank broker exceptions (GLBA)12 CFR 218
SReimbursement for producing financial records12 CFR 219
TMargin credit — brokers12 CFR 220
UMargin credit — banks12 CFR 221
WAffiliate transactions, sections 23A and 23B12 CFR 223
YBank holding companies and change in control12 CFR 225
EENetting eligibility12 CFR 231
GGUnlawful internet gambling (UIGEA)12 CFR 233

Post-Dodd-Frank prudential

Mostly large-bank and systemic-risk rules.

RegSubjectCFR
HHDesignated financial market utilities12 CFR 234
IIDebit interchange and routing (Durbin)12 CFR 235
KKFederal assistance to swaps entities (swaps pushout)12 CFR 237
LLSavings and loan holding companies12 CFR 238
MMMutual holding companies12 CFR 239
NNRetail forex transactions12 CFR 240
OOSecurities holding companies12 CFR 241
PPDodd-Frank Title I definitions12 CFR 242
QQResolution plans (living wills)12 CFR 243
RRCredit risk retention12 CFR 244
TTSupervision and regulation assessment fees12 CFR 246
VVVolcker Rule12 CFR 248
WWLiquidity Coverage Ratio12 CFR 249
XXConcentration limits12 CFR 251
YYEnhanced prudential standards12 CFR 252

Built by Alexander Smith, CRCM, CFE
Risk Advisory Services · Kaufman Rossin
I built this to keep track of federal regulatory activity affecting community banks and fintechs. If your institution is tracking this manually — or wants monitoring shaped around its own risk profile — I'm happy to talk it through.
Connect on LinkedIn Email me Full bio
Deadlines are structured fields from matched Federal Register documents. Summaries are model-generated from agency listings and are not a substitute for reading the source document. Not legal or compliance advice.
